Products/ Stabilisers/ Carry Flow
Stabilisers

Carry Flow

Coated xanthan gum polymer for suspension, carrying power, and borehole cleaning in water-based drilling fluids.

Product Overview

What Carry Flow does

Carry Flow is a powdered xanthan gum with a coating that makes it easier to mix. It improves suspension, carrying power, and borehole cleaning in water-based drilling fluids — freshwater, seawater, salt, and brine.

When the pumps stop, it builds a gel and holds cuttings up. When you start pumping, it loosens up and moves. At low flow, it stays thick enough to keep cuttings suspended until you're circulating again.

Carry Flow is the most used polymer in the Blick Fluids range — it appears in more fluid systems than any other product.

Key Benefits

Superior cuttings suspension and transport
Coated powder for easy, lump-free mixing
Works in freshwater, seawater, and brine
Gels at rest, flows when pumping — holds cuttings in place
Technical Details

Specifications

Appearance White to off-white powder
Specific Gravity 1.6
Bulk Density 800 kg/m³
Recommended Treatment 1 to 6 kg/m³ (0.35 to 2.1 lb/bbl). Add slowly through the hopper.
Packaging 15kg pails

Downloads

Safety Data Sheet (SDS) PDF Product Sheet PDF
Core Fluid Systems

Where Carry Flow is used

Ground conditions covered by Carry Flow across drilling segments.

HDD 4 / 5
Sticky Clay
Sand Gravel & Firm
Heavy Rock & Gravel
Large Diameter Sand/Rock
Non-Reactive (Large)
Open fluid selector
Waterwell 2 / 4
Sand
Reactive Clay
Heavy Lifting
Hole Cleaning (Foam)
Open fluid selector
Fluid System

Build your fluid system with Carry Flow

Products commonly used alongside Carry Flow in drilling fluid systems.

Carrying Capacity
Carry Max
High-yield polymer for maximum carrying capacity and cuttings suspension in heavy ground conditions.
Carrying Capacity
Carry Foam
Foaming surfactant for lightweight cuttings transport and hole cleaning in large diameter or dry conditions.
The Foundation
Max Bore Wyo Ben
Premium Wyoming bentonite for building the base fluid system — provides viscosity, fluid loss control, and borehole stability.
